name: alpha_regions
description: alpha region catalogue — coarse, illustrative footprints for named ecosystems across alpha's five valuation biomes. These are NOT authoritative boundaries; they are simplified polygons used to drive the map overlays and the Total Ecosystem Value comparison view. Each feature's biome_key maps to reference_data.BIOMES, and each polygon is valued server-side via valuation.compute_valuation. Refresh footprints from the authoritative sources noted below.

source.primary: Olson, D.M. et al. (2001). Terrestrial Ecoregions of the World. BioScience 51(11): 933-938. WWF/TEOW.
source.mangrove: Giri, C. et al. (2011). Global mangrove forests distribution (USGS/MEoW).
source.note: Simplified, low-resolution footprints for in-app display and valuation; not survey-grade boundaries.

properties.name: Amazon Basin
properties.region: Brazil, Peru, Colombia, Bolivia, Ecuador, Venezuela
properties.gdp_callout: Standing, this basin generates more annual economic value than the entire cattle-ranching sector that drives its clearance in Brazil.

properties.name: Congo Basin
properties.region: DRC, Republic of Congo, Cameroon, Gabon
properties.gdp_callout: Standing, this basin generates more annual economic value than the timber-export industry that threatens it across Central Africa.

properties.name: Southeast Asian Rainforests
properties.region: Indonesia, Malaysia, Papua New Guinea, Borneo
properties.gdp_callout: Standing, these forests generate more annual economic value than the palm-oil plantations replacing them across the region.

properties.name: Central American Rainforests
properties.gdp_callout: Standing, these forests generate more annual economic value than the agricultural conversion pressuring them in the isthmus.

properties.name: Atlantic Forest
properties.gdp_callout: What survives of this forest still out-earns the sugarcane and pasture that replaced 85% of it along Brazil's coast.

properties.name: Western Ghats
properties.gdp_callout: These hills water peninsular India; standing, they out-value the plantations creeping up their slopes.

properties.name: New Guinea Rainforests
properties.gdp_callout: One of the last great intact tropical wildernesses — worth more whole than logged for tropical hardwood.

properties.name: Sundarbans
properties.gdp_callout: The world's largest mangrove forest shields tens of millions from cyclones — a storm barrier no sea wall could afford to replace.

properties.name: Niger Delta Mangroves
properties.gdp_callout: Africa's largest mangrove belt buries blue carbon and breeds the fisheries the delta lives on — value oil spills keep erasing.

properties.name: Mesoamerican Reef Mangroves
properties.gdp_callout: These coastal forests nurse the reef fisheries and absorb the hurricanes that batter the Yucatán — worth more rooted than cleared for resorts.

properties.name: Borneo Coastal Mangroves
properties.gdp_callout: Standing, these tidal forests out-earn the shrimp ponds and plantations carved into them.

properties.name: Pantanal
properties.gdp_callout: The world's largest tropical wetland filters a continent's water and buffers its floods — services no treatment plant could price.

properties.name: Okavango Delta
properties.gdp_callout: An inland delta that turns desert into one of Africa's richest wildlife economies — worth far more wet than drained.

properties.name: Everglades
properties.gdp_callout: This 'river of grass' recharges South Florida's drinking water and blunts its storm surge — replacing it would cost billions a year.

properties.name: Sudd Wetlands
properties.gdp_callout: One of the world's largest freshwater wetlands sustains pastoral economies and the White Nile's flow downstream.

properties.name: Pacific Northwest Forests
properties.gdp_callout: These temperate rainforests store more carbon per hectare than almost any forest on earth — worth more standing than milled.

properties.name: Central European Forests
properties.gdp_callout: Europe's mixed forests cool its cities and clean its watersheds — natural infrastructure worth more than the timber yield.

properties.name: Valdivian Forests
properties.gdp_callout: South America's only temperate rainforest harbours ancient endemics — standing, it out-earns the pulp plantations replacing it.

properties.name: Appalachian Forests
properties.gdp_callout: These ancient mountains hold the richest temperate broadleaf diversity on the continent — and the watersheds that supply its cities.

properties.name: Ussuri Broadleaf Forests
properties.gdp_callout: The Amur tiger's last forest stores carbon and anchors a transboundary watershed worth more intact than logged.

properties.name: North American Great Plains
properties.gdp_callout: Beneath the prairie lies one of earth's great soil-carbon stores — worth more rooted than plowed into the next dust bowl.

properties.name: Eurasian Steppe
properties.gdp_callout: The world's largest grassland holds vast soil carbon and pasture economies — value released to the air when it is tilled.

properties.name: Pampas
properties.gdp_callout: South America's fertile grassland feeds the world's beef — but its soil nutrient bank is worth more conserved than mined.

properties.name: Mongolian Grasslands
properties.gdp_callout: One of the last intact temperate grasslands sustains nomadic herding and a deep soil-carbon reserve.

properties.name: South African Highveld
properties.gdp_callout: The Highveld grasslands feed the rivers that supply Johannesburg — natural water infrastructure worth more than the cropland that displaces it.

properties.name: Siberian Taiga
properties.gdp_callout: The taiga is the planet's largest land carbon store — worth more standing than logged for softwood or cleared for extraction.

properties.name: Canadian Boreal Forest
properties.gdp_callout: Canada's boreal forest filters a continent's water and locks away carbon worth more than the pulp and mining leases carving into it.

properties.name: Fennoscandian Taiga
properties.gdp_callout: Standing, these northern forests bank carbon and clean water worth more than the timber rotations replacing them.

properties.name: US Corn Belt
properties.gdp_callout: Even as cropland, this soil holds nutrient and carbon value that simply vanishes when it is paved for warehouses and subdivisions.

properties.name: North European Plain Farmland
properties.gdp_callout: Europe's breadbasket soils are living infrastructure — worth defending for the water and carbon they hold, not just this year's harvest.

properties.name: Punjab Croplands
properties.gdp_callout: Beneath the wheat lies a soil-and-water system worth more maintained than mined by over-extraction and sprawl.

properties.name: Laurentian Great Lakes
properties.gdp_callout: One-fifth of the world's surface fresh water — a drinking-water reserve no treatment plant could ever replace.

properties.name: Lake Baikal
properties.gdp_callout: The world's deepest lake holds a fifth of its unfrozen fresh water; its filtration and habitat value is effectively priceless.

properties.name: Lake Victoria
properties.gdp_callout: Africa's great lake waters and feeds tens of millions — a freshwater asset worth far more than the pollution degrading it.

properties.name: London Green Belt
properties.gdp_callout: The green belt cools the capital, soaks up its floods and feeds its rivers — value that disappears the day it is built over.

properties.name: Randstad Green Heart
properties.gdp_callout: The open land between the Dutch cities buffers floods and stores carbon — worth far more than another logistics park.

properties.name: Rhine-Ruhr Open Land
properties.gdp_callout: The open land threading Europe's densest conurbation is natural infrastructure — quietly worth more green than sealed.
